qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via malicious Egg import script_install field
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes code injection via malicious Egg import scripts in Pterodactyl Panel, while advisory describes websocket rate limiting DoS in Pterodactyl Wings. Different vulnerable components, different root causes (missing script validation vs missing rate limits), different attack vectors (admin file upload vs websocket connection flood), and different vulnerability classes (CWE-94 code injection vs CWE-400/CWE-770 resource consumption).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes missing rate limiting and message size limits on websocket endpoints in Pterodactyl Wings (Go) leading to DoS via resource exhaustion. The qualifying finding describes code injection via malicious Egg import scripts in Pterodactyl Panel (PHP/Laravel), which is a different vulnerability class, different component, and different root cause from the advisory-described websocket rate limiting issue.
evidence: Advisory target: github.com/pterodactyl/wings (Go) websocket endpoints lacking rate limits (CWE-400, CWE-770)
Qualifying finding (egg_import_rce): Pterodactyl Panel PHP code injection via Egg import scripts - different component, different root cause, different vulnerability class
